fields.description:
## Why
Humans and a future CI gate need to see/consume all attestations on a ticket.
## What
Render the `attestations` map in `show` and the MCP ticket-state response (omitted when
empty). NOTE: the HMAC-hex strip for `attestations.<kind>.signature` is implemented in 114f
(it introduces the two-level shape, so the strip lands with it — no leak window); this story
only ASSERTS the rendered output carries no hex for any kind. Document the model in
`docs/plan-review-gate.md` (a new attestations section): when each kind is emitted,
additive-across/replace-within semantics, immutable-record + `compute_validity` on read, the
legacy `signature` mirror (back-compat; its removal is the deferred follow-up
352b-5097-9971-4dc1), and how a future CI gate consumes `verify-signature --kind K`.
Document the HMAC symmetric-key constraint (a CI verifier needs `REBAR_SIGNING_KEY`) as
known follow-up; do NOT build a CI `gate-check` command here. SCOPE BOUNDARY: this story
owns the `attestations` field in the ticket-state MCP response; the `verify_signature` MCP
tool + schema are owned by cc1b.
## Scope
`src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py` / show path, `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` (ticket-state
response only), `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, `CLAUDE.md` attestations note.
## Depends on
Requires 114f (state shape + hex strip) and cc1b (verify surface) merged.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `show`/MCP render `attestations` (kind -> record); omitted when empty.
- [ ] Output carries NO HMAC hex for any kind (the strip itself lands in 114f; verified here).
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` documents emission per kind, semantics, `compute_validity`, the legacy mirror + its deferred removal (352b).
- [ ] CI-readiness (per-kind certified check) + the HMAC symmetric-key constraint documented as follow-up; no CI `gate-check` command added.
